svg.text: Service-owned compatibility subsystem · Core closed-schema · compiled-in plugins · bounded hot path
• identity DTO publish to read-model seam
NO vendor state · NO DP parsing · NO quirks
MQTT bridge · Matter bridge · Web API
Tuya DPs map into these existing kinds
matches device using manufacturer + model + clusters
Input: manufacturer, model, endpoints, cluster list, DP hints
TuyaPayloadView: pointer + length + endpoint + cluster + mfr_code
Lifetime: valid only within HAL callback scope
Output: TuyaDpParseResult (typed DP items, bounded count)
calls selected plugin · converts output to Service-level DTOs
4. Applies semantic dedup/coalescing per plugin hints
vendor-specific init state machine — outside Core
Actions: time sync · manufacturer handshake · retry/ack
3. HAL adapter: ZCL encode OR Tuya DP encode/send
plugin = behavior module / strategy | quirk data = per-model declarative data used by that behavior
Stage 1: compiled-in constexpr tables | Stage 2: FS/NVS-backed · codegen from YAML/JSON at build time
Parser, translator, and plugin engine must not know where quirk data is physically stored
codegen: YAML/JSON → constexpr C++ tables at build time · execution engine in code · complex models keep custom hooks
One device may use both standard ZCL path + Tuya 0xEF00 path · fingerprinting enables capability composition
✗ publish to MQTT / Matter / Web directly
✗ send Zigbee frames bypassing HAL adapter
• Type system: plugin API accepts/returns only permitted types
• check_arch_invariants.sh: tuya_* files must not include core_*.hpp, hal_*.h, mqtt_*.hpp
• ServiceRuntime owns final Core-event construction boundary
Accept: no Core change, host tests pass
5. Add one compiled-in plugin + quirk data
Accept: init state outside Core snapshot
✓ parsed DPs map into existing domain events
✓ host tests: parser, translator, plugin, quirk
Tuya Implementation Architecture · zigbee-gateway_v1 · Core closed-schema · Service-owned compatibility subsystem
